$comment: Neutral shipped <limits.h> (Item 1, 2026-06-22). C 5.2.4.2.1 integer width macros. A C `.h` would be language-specific TEXT and couple the shipped config to C; instead each object-like `#define` that IS a compile-time constant is expressed as a NEUTRAL typed named constant (name + value + hir-text type) and INJECTED by the semantic phase, exactly like the descriptor's extern symbols. The constant folds to a literal at HIR lowering AND in constant-expression position (`int a[CHAR_BIT]`). No `symbols`/`library` — <limits.h> has no linkable surface, only macros.
